test.notes: Tests pass but some evidence_pipeline tests take 60+ seconds each (test_evidence_pipeline_chunking, test_evidence_pipeline_process). Tests did not fail, just slow.

blockingIssues.description: Duplicate SearchDepth enum defined in agents.rs when search_router.rs already has a more sophisticated version. ResearcherAgent::classify_depth() is a simplified static version that doesn't reuse SearchRouter::classify_depth().

ResearchResult, ResearchFinding, and SearchDepth are not exported from the orchestrator crate's lib.rs. Other agent result types (EvaluationResult, ReviewResult, RefactorResult, etc.) are exported. This breaks discoverability.
max_iterations is hardcoded to 5 in all builder methods. This magic number should be loaded from .swell/settings.json per project conventions in AGENTS.md.
Web search tools (WebSearchTool, DomainSearchTool, FetchPageTool) exist in web_search.rs but are not registered in swell-tools/src/mod.rs. ResearcherAgent cannot access these tools.

Web search tools are implemented but never registered in ToolRegistry. The feature expectedBehavior states 'Tools registered in ToolRegistry and available to ALL agents', but no registration code exists.
ResearcherAgent does not have web search tools instantiated or registered in its with_tools builder. The agent has no access to web search tools despite the feature description.

retrieve() method creates EvidenceResult with empty chunk data. The chunk field is populated with EvidenceChunk containing String::new() for text and empty provenance fields. The actual chunks stored in the pipeline's inverted_index are never looked up and returned. This makes retrieve() completely unusable.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document how to register tools in ToolRegistry. The web search tools feature shows workers are implementing tools but not registering them. AGENTS.md should clarify: (1) how tools get registered, (2) that all tools must be registered to be accessible, (3) the pattern for registering tools in registry.rs vs elsewhere.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: research-web-search-tools review: 'Worker implemented web search tools as new module but AGENTS.md has no documentation about how tools should be registered in ToolRegistry'

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify when to reuse existing types vs create new ones. research-researcher-agent created a duplicate SearchDepth enum instead of reusing search_router::SearchDepth. AGENTS.md should include guidance: 'Before creating new types, search the codebase for existing equivalents'.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: research-researcher-agent review: 'SearchRouter in search_router.rs has sophisticated query classification... ResearcherAgent creates a simpler static classify_depth() that checks different conditions... The existence of SearchRouter wasn't leveraged'

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update skill to clarify error handling: SwellError from swell_core is the project's standard error type (wraps thiserror internally). Workers should use SwellError rather than creating custom thiserror types. The current skill mentions thiserror::Error which may mislead workers.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: research-researcher-agent review sharedStateObservations: 'Worker skill (rust) says to use thiserror::Error for errors, but SwellError is used directly from swell_core'
